scenarios.description: At pest control franchise (25 users, mobile-first), a Play ransomware attack is in progress. Initial access was gained via compromised legitimate remote support session. The attacker has achieved the following: lateral movement to 7 network segments. Demand: 2 BTC ($180K). Technique aligns with T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery).

scenarios.time_context: Monday holiday (Labor Day) — long weekend
scenarios.description: Confirmed data breach at credit union (85 users, regulated). Exposed: architectural blueprints and bid proposals (340 patient files). Cause: screenshot of records posted on social media. Compliance note: Regulatory impact assessment needed.

scenarios.environment: mid-size law firm (80 users, hybrid cloud)
scenarios.time_context: Christmas Eve — half staff, early close
scenarios.description: Unauthorized access discovered at mid-size law firm (80 users, hybrid cloud) — evidence suggests access for 70 days. Method: Kerberoasting — cracked service account password. Scope of data accessed is still being determined.

scenarios.time_context: during annual security audit
scenarios.description: Phishing campaign targeting pest control franchise (25 users, mobile-first): 18 users received emails. Lure: Zoom meeting invite with credential harvester link. 1 user(s) interacted. Result: MFA fatigue attack succeeded — user approved push.

scenarios.environment: mid-size law firm (80 users, hybrid cloud)
scenarios.time_context: Christmas Eve — half staff, early close
scenarios.description: Denial of service at mid-size law firm (80 users, hybrid cloud). Attack type: application-layer HTTP flood. Impact: client-facing portal unreachable.

scenarios.time_context: 30 minutes before court filing deadline
scenarios.description: Web application attack at small medical practice (15 users, 2 servers). Attack: path traversal accessing /etc/passwd. Target: student information system (SIS).

scenarios.time_context: Thursday 6:00 AM — before first shift arrives
scenarios.description: Phishing campaign targeting funeral home group (20 users, 4 locations): 43 users received emails. Lure: fake browser update popup on legitimate site. 2 user(s) interacted. At least one led to: reverse shell established via PowerShell.

scenarios.time_context: Sunday 9:00 PM — night before major deadline
scenarios.description: Denial of service at veterinary hospital (30 users, 3 clinics). Attack type: volumetric DDoS — 45 Gbps UDP flood. Impact: online scheduling system down during peak booking hours.

scenarios.time_context: Monday 8:15 AM — just after login rush
scenarios.description: Phishing campaign targeting school district (500 users, 12 campuses): 75 users received emails. Lure: urgent wire transfer approval from CFO. 7 user(s) interacted. At least one led to: email forwarding rule silently exfiltrating mail.
